ghost.title: The Ghost of Times
ghost.motto: Written by those who lived it. Reporting for those who do.
ghost.blurb: A daily paper in which writers across the centuries — Carlyle, Chesterton, Hemingway, Whitman, Jung and the rest — each file one op-ed on the news of the day.

fingerprint.title: The Fingerprint
fingerprint.kicker: The global programmatic & Advanced TV market paper
fingerprint.motto: All the news that's fit to Fingerprint
fingerprint.blurb: An independent daily wire on the global programmatic and Advanced TV (CTV) market — platforms, performance, partnerships, competition, regulation, political-ad spend, and a five-market global desk, with closing op-eds in the voices of McLuhan and Ogilvy.

ancestry-research.title: The Emigrant's Atlas
ancestry-research.subtitle: Two families — Collins and Pokorný — and the long road from Munster and Galicia to America.

carlyle-french-revolution-research.title: The Book That Burned
carlyle-french-revolution-research.subtitle: Carlyle's French Revolution: the manuscript a maid destroyed, rewritten from memory into the history that gave us A Tale of Two Cities.

carlyle-friedrich-research.title: Carlyle's Everest
carlyle-friedrich-research.subtitle: A close reading of Friedrich the Great — the prose, the battle-pieces, and the long, contested afterlife of Carlyle's biggest book.

carlyle-research.title: The Calvinist Without a Creed
carlyle-research.subtitle: How a lost Scottish Calvinism became the gospel of work, hero-worship, and prophetic fury — and why Carlyle is still fought over.

civil-war-religion-whitman-research.title: A Holy War and Its Poet
civil-war-religion-whitman-research.subtitle: How religion made the Civil War possible — and how Walt Whitman turned its carnage into America's civil religion.

deutsch-good-explanations-research.title: Hard to Vary
deutsch-good-explanations-research.subtitle: David Deutsch's test for a good explanation — and what it does to science, theology, politics, and the media plan.

ipv4-ipv6-ctv-research.title: The Address Knows You
ipv4-ipv6-ctv-research.subtitle: How the humble IP address — IPv4, CGNAT, IPv6 — quietly became the identity layer of streaming-TV advertising, and what is breaking it.

jesus-research.title: The Man and the Christ
jesus-research.subtitle: Jesus as historical figure, philosopher, and divine Son — read through Carlyle, Whitman, Jung, and Peirce, then set on one table.

john-the-baptist-research.title: More Than a Forerunner
john-the-baptist-research.subtitle: John the Baptist as history, theology, and myth — the prophet who baptized Jesus, the sect that still follows him, and the need such figures fill.

jung-research.title: A God in the Psyche
jung-research.subtitle: Jung's lifelong wrestle with the self, the soul, and God — the numinous, Answer to Job, synchronicity, alchemy, and where Peterson reads him right and wrong.

phenomenology-pragmatism-research.title: Experience and Consequences
phenomenology-pragmatism-research.subtitle: The two great modern philosophies of experience and action — and what they did to psychology and Christianity, from Husserl and James to 2040.

uap-research.title: From Roswell to Disclosure
uap-research.subtitle: The American UFO question from 1947 to the disclosure era — the institutions, the evidence and its limits, the Grusch testimony, and where it heads next.

us-geopolitics-research.title: The Map of American Power
us-geopolitics-research.subtitle: The structural logic of US grand strategy in 2026 — alliances, rivalries, the pivot to Latin America, and a forecast for Taiwan, Ukraine, Iran, and the chip war.
